整套首页
unit_19 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_19

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 392 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC SINGLE
Confounding the perception of needlework as __________, the Oxburgh embroideries, executed by a sixteenth-century Scottish queen and her attendant, incorporate text and imagery from books by distinguished scholars of the time.Confounding the perception of needlework as __________, the Oxburgh embroideries, executed by a sixteenth-century Scottish queen and her attendant, incorporate text and imagery from books by distinguished scholars of the time.
答案：E　a vacuous pastime
refinedelitistedifying
refined: standard unit headword | elitist: standard unit headword | edifying: standard unit headword
#2 SE
Critics of compost production often do not fully consider the potential environmental benefits of compost use - benefits that might __________ the energy consumption, gaseous emissions, and other negative environmental effects attributable to compost production.Critics of compost production often do not fully consider the potential environmental benefits of compost use - benefits that might __________ the energy consumption, gaseous emissions, and other negative environmental effects attributable to compost production.
答案：B, F　offset; counterbalance
offsetproductionemission
offset: standard unit headword | production: standard unit headword | emission: standard unit headword
#3 TC DOUBLE
Not only is the field of behavioral genetics strewn with (i)__________ finding, but even among those findings managed to survive, many have turned out to be (ii)__________ a very restricted class of cases.Not only is the field of behavioral genetics strewn with (i)__________ finding, but even among those findings managed to survive, many have turned out to be (ii)__________ a very restricted class of cases.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　repudiated; germane to
geneticgenetics
genetic: standard unit headword | genetics: standard unit headword
#4 TC TRIPLE
Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)__________ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)__________, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community's (iii)__________ that much more devastating.Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)__________ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)__________, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community's (iii)__________ that much more devastating.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　enterprising; productive; impotence
resourcefuldevastating
resourceful: standard unit headword | devastating: standard unit headword
#5 SE
Given the __________ of solid case studies of environmental degradation, this new case study adds little to our knowledge of the field.Given the __________ of solid case studies of environmental degradation, this new case study adds little to our knowledge of the field.
答案：B, C　plethora; surfeit
solidgiven
solid: standard unit headword | given: standard unit headword

The enthusiasm that many English artists and writers felt regarding the Labour Party (i)__________ amid complaints that arts funding remained as (ii)__________. And even though the government recently announced a significant funding boost for the Arts Council, some art leaders still refuse to (iii)__________ Labour.The enthusiasm that many English artists and writers felt regarding the Labour Party (i)__________ amid complaints that arts funding remained as (ii)__________. And even though the government recently announced a significant funding boost for the Arts Council, some art leaders still refuse to (iii)__________ Labour.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　waned; parsimonious; trust
waneparsimonious
wane: standard unit headword | parsimonious: standard unit headword
#7 TC SINGLE
The combination of Isabella Beeton’s short life and the __________ of evidence for some parts of her story means that at times her biographer, Kathryn Hughes, is forced to fill out her narrative with background information.The combination of Isabella Beeton’s short life and the __________ of evidence for some parts of her story means that at times her biographer, Kathryn Hughes, is forced to fill out her narrative with background information.
答案：D　paucity
forcenarrative
force: standard unit headword | narrative: standard unit headword
#8 TC SINGLE
If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition since the plan would then involve __________ a historic population, not trying to build a population from scratch.If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition since the plan would then involve __________ a historic population, not trying to build a population from scratch.
答案：B　augmenting
endemicopposition
endemic: standard unit headword | opposition: standard unit headword

Their joy in movement is so pure, so complete, and so (ii) __________.Despite having only recently learned to walk, toddlers make the most (i) __________ dance students. Their joy in movement is so pure, so complete, and so (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　delightful; contagious
contagiousfutile
contagious: standard unit headword | futile: standard unit headword
#10 SE
Any antimatter in our part of the universe is necessarily __________ because of the overwhelming preponderance of ordinary matter, by which antimatter is quickly annihilated.Any antimatter in our part of the universe is necessarily __________ because of the overwhelming preponderance of ordinary matter, by which antimatter is quickly annihilated.
答案：A, F　short-lived; ephemeral
preponderanceoverwhelming
preponderance: standard unit headword | overwhelming: standard unit headword

One contemporary observed the (ii) __________ of the painter to his social milieu: its meretricious taste was well served by his (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derision; appositeness; willingness to flatter

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　everyday presence; pedestrian

This novel is not great literature; there are too many characters, too many subplots, and no clearly defined protagonist, making for __________ story.This novel is not great literature; there are too many characters, too many subplots, and no clearly defined protagonist, making for __________ story.
答案：B, E　a diffuse; an amorphous
mawkishbanal

He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. He is among the most uninhibited comic performers around, but here he buttons his lip and stares straight ahead. Perhaps without quite knowing it, the audience waits for a wink, a hint that some of the (ii) __________ spirit that animated his previous movies might be lurking inside the (iii) __________ manner he presents in this film.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　forbearance; madcap; reserved
beguilelurkedifying
beguile: standard unit headword | lurk: standard unit headword | edifying: standard unit headword
#15 TC SINGLE
While some mathematicians regard word problems as a useful pedagogical tool, others argue that they tend to be so __________ that, paradoxically, they give the impression that mathematics has no relevance to the real world.While some mathematicians regard word problems as a useful pedagogical tool, others argue that they tend to be so __________ that, paradoxically, they give the impression that mathematics has no relevance to the real world.
答案：B　contrived
pedagogicalcontrive
pedagogical: standard unit headword | contrive: standard unit headword
#16 TC TRIPLE
For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i) __________. Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii) __________ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii) __________ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry's bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i) __________. Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii) __________ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii) __________ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry's bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　sacrifice; relinquishing; humility
demisepiety
demise: standard unit headword | piety: standard unit headword

Is there a contemporary filmmaker who is genuinely devoted to great literature? In this age of puerile Hollywood productions, it may be (i) __________ a few serious intellectuals from the roster of glib, lowbrow directors. But to find a director (ii) __________ great books--and able to make them into great movies--takes some searching.Is there a contemporary filmmaker who is genuinely devoted to great literature? In this age of puerile Hollywood productions, it may be (i) __________ a few serious intellectuals from the roster of glib, lowbrow directors. But to find a director (ii) __________ great books--and able to make them into great movies--takes some searching.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　possible to extract; committed to
puerileproduction
puerile: standard unit headword | production: standard unit headword
#18 SE
While recognizing that recent reports of cyberwarfare, phone-hacking scandals, and identity thefts have tended to accent the destructive connotation of the word, Sue Halpern maintains that “hacking” is such __________ term that its meaning nearly always derives from its context.While recognizing that recent reports of cyberwarfare, phone-hacking scandals, and identity thefts have tended to accent the destructive connotation of the word, Sue Halpern maintains that “hacking” is such __________ term that its meaning nearly always derives from its context.
答案：A, B　a generic; an inclusive
connotation
connotation: standard unit headword

Readers looking for another condemnation of private equity firms should look elsewhere, for this book is not __________ such firms.Readers looking for another condemnation of private equity firms should look elsewhere, for this book is not __________ such firms.
答案：A, B　a rant against; a diatribe on
rantdisquisition
rant: standard unit headword | disquisition: standard unit headword
#20 TC SINGLE
In the last two hundred years, the practice of archaeology has changed greatly, from digging up ancient artifacts for use by wealthy individuals as art objects to analyzing the detritus of everyday life in the laboratory, and thus from __________ to data collection.In the last two hundred years, the practice of archaeology has changed greatly, from digging up ancient artifacts for use by wealthy individuals as art objects to analyzing the detritus of everyday life in the laboratory, and thus from __________ to data collection.
答案：D　treasure hunting
archaeology
archaeology: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　aimlessness; discursions
irk

Not only was Pushkin’s personal correspondence intercepted and his movements (iii)__________, but Tsar Nicholas I’s decision to oversee Pushkin’s career obliged Pushkin to submit all his manuscripts for inspection.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　struck by; suffocating lack of creative freedom; monitored
suffocate
suffocate: standard unit headword

答案：D　outstrips
eclipse
eclipse: standard unit headword

In a competitive news market, producers can use slant to differentiate their products and stave off price competition.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　accuracy; bias
slant

We (i)__________ the idea that personal politics are entirely (ii)__________. The genetic explanation for ideology (iii)__________ our belief that we are persuaded only by rational arguments.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　revel in; deliberative; defies

答案：B, D　averted; forestalled

答案：A　significance

Blank 2

答案：B, C　disparate; heterogeneous

Blank 3

答案：A, D　preachy; sanctimonious

Some ambitious lawyers are willing to work on Supreme Court cases without charge in an effort to gain __________; they believe that this increased cachet will help them succeed in the future.Some ambitious lawyers are willing to work on Supreme Court cases without charge in an effort to gain __________; they believe that this increased cachet will help them succeed in the future.
答案：C　prestige

But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii) __________: our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii) __________ are vital to ecological health. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii) __________ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay
crumble

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　spontaneity; immediacy; improvisation

答案：A　jejune

Philosophy is generally considered (i) __________ business in that it is pursued by thinkers constructing and evaluating arguments according to dispassionate considerations of soundness. In this view, the flesh-and-blood men and women involved--their personalities and histories--are (ii) __________ their philosophical claims. Yet some mavericks argue that philosophical positions are (iii) __________ the individuals who hold them and are enriched by biographical information.Philosophy is generally considered (i) __________ business in that it is pursued by thinkers constructing and evaluating arguments according to dispassionate considerations of soundness. In this view, the flesh-and-blood men and women involved--their personalities and histories--are (ii) __________ their philosophical claims. Yet some mavericks argue that philosophical positions are (iii) __________ the individuals who hold them and are enriched by biographical information.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　an impersonal; irrelevant to; inseparable from
maverick
maverick: standard unit headword

答案：A, C　senescence; decrepitude
decrepitude

Their forms thus necessarily become (i) __________ over time and their original features are (ii) __________.

Blank 2
答案：B, F　degraded; obscured

答案：E, F　mitigated; abated

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　umbrage; collectively overthrow
torpor
torpor: standard unit headword
#52 TC SINGLE
The stars' attitudes toward their glowing press were diametrically opposed, one seeing it as __________ and the other taking the adulation seriously.The stars' attitudes toward their glowing press were diametrically opposed, one seeing it as __________ and the other taking the adulation seriously.
答案：D　flummery
flummery
flummery: standard unit headword
#53 SE
The uniquely human ability to rethink and revise our social arrangements is a weird blessing, allowing us to create systems that are as likely to __________ us as to liberate us.The uniquely human ability to rethink and revise our social arrangements is a weird blessing, allowing us to create systems that are as likely to __________ us as to liberate us.
答案：B, E　shackle; stifle
admonish
admonish: standard unit headword
#54 TC SINGLE
My grandma has a strong belief in all things __________: she insists, for example, that the house in which she lived as a child was haunted.My grandma has a strong belief in all things __________: she insists, for example, that the house in which she lived as a child was haunted.
答案：C　numinous
invidious
invidious: standard unit headword
#55 TC DOUBLE
Many of the major developments in fundamental physics of the past century arose from (i) __________ existing ideas. For example, the (ii) __________ Maxwell's equations and Galilean invariance led Einstein to propose the special theory of relativity. Similarly, the failure to reconcile special relativity with Newtonian gravity led him to develop the general theory of relativity.Many of the major developments in fundamental physics of the past century arose from (i) __________ existing ideas. For example, the (ii) __________ Maxwell's equations and Galilean invariance led Einstein to propose the special theory of relativity. Similarly, the failure to reconcile special relativity with Newtonian gravity led him to develop the general theory of relativity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　overcoming contradictions between; incompatibility of
purview
purview: standard unit headword
#56 TC TRIPLE
To read Joanna Scott is to admire the work of a (i) __________. From sentence to story, she narrates with great skill and (ii) __________, so that the reader soon relaxes in the assurance that a hint or a brushstroke delivered in chapter 1 will be (iii) __________ before the novel comes to an end.To read Joanna Scott is to admire the work of a (i) __________. From sentence to story, she narrates with great skill and (ii) __________, so that the reader soon relaxes in the assurance that a hint or a brushstroke delivered in chapter 1 will be (iii) __________ before the novel comes to an end.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　master; deliberation; given import

#57 SE
In the absence of a surface gradient, the new laws of refraction and reflection are __________ the conventional law, so they represent more of an extension than a complete revolution.In the absence of a surface gradient, the new laws of refraction and reflection are __________ the conventional law, so they represent more of an extension than a complete revolution.
答案：A, B　inferable from; entailed by
antecedent
antecedent: standard unit headword
#58 TC TRIPLE
In 1987, Japanese chemists demonstrated that tiny particles of gold could act as (i) __________ facilitating the combination of carbon monoxide and oxygen into carbon dioxide. The idea that gold could hasten chemical reactions was (ii) __________: after all, gold is (iii) __________ in its bulk form. It has been used for millennia to make jewelry precisely because it does not tarnish by reacting with oxygen or water.In 1987, Japanese chemists demonstrated that tiny particles of gold could act as (i) __________ facilitating the combination of carbon monoxide and oxygen into carbon dioxide. The idea that gold could hasten chemical reactions was (ii) __________: after all, gold is (iii) __________ in its bulk form. It has been used for millennia to make jewelry precisely because it does not tarnish by reacting with oxygen or water.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　a catalyst; counterintuitive; inert
discouraging
discouraging: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　sterling reputation of; exhaustive

The era spanning the collapse of the Western Roman Empire up to the tenth century was a rich and creative period in the development of Europe and thus deserves a less __________ designation than the “Dark Ages” label it customarily receives.The era spanning the collapse of the Western Roman Empire up to the tenth century was a rich and creative period in the development of Europe and thus deserves a less __________ designation than the “Dark Ages” label it customarily receives.
答案：C　dismissive

Culture, like speech, is primarily a human faculty, although both functions may exist in a more __________ form in lesser primates.Culture, like speech, is primarily a human faculty, although both functions may exist in a more __________ form in lesser primates.
答案：C, F　primitive; rudimentary

intelligible: standard unit headword
#62 TC TRIPLE
Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) __________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) __________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) __________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) __________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) __________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) __________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　subvert; ecumenical; a pastiche of styles
ecumenicalparsimonious
ecumenical: standard unit headword | parsimonious: standard unit headword

Williamson had a fierce commitment to achieving an accord, spending enormous amount of time trying to forge a consensus out of an often __________ assembly.Williamson had a fierce commitment to achieving an accord, spending enormous amount of time trying to forge a consensus out of an often __________ assembly.
答案：B, C　fractious; restive

compliant: standard unit headword

Teachers in sports, music, and other fields often believe that natural aptitude matters a great deal, but sometimes they may mistake (i) __________ for (ii) __________: there is no way to tell, from a recital alone, whether a young violinist's extraordinary performance stems from years of training or from (iii) __________.Teachers in sports, music, and other fields often believe that natural aptitude matters a great deal, but sometimes they may mistake (i) __________ for (ii) __________: there is no way to tell, from a recital alone, whether a young violinist's extraordinary performance stems from years of training or from (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　diligence; talent; innate ability

The environmental advocacy group pushed for a single, overarching wetlands management plan that would __________ the existing efforts of various entities, resulting in a focused blueprint for saving the area’s wetlands.The environmental advocacy group pushed for a single, overarching wetlands management plan that would __________ the existing efforts of various entities, resulting in a focused blueprint for saving the area’s wetlands.
答案：A, B　combine; meld

答案：B, D　unlinked; estranged

estrange: standard unit headword

Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious

The initial, widely shared pessimism turned out to be __________, because it ignored the many things that would be done with resources left behind.The initial, widely shared pessimism turned out to be __________, because it ignored the many things that would be done with resources left behind.
答案：D, F　unfounded; unjustified

答案：E, F　tempered; modulated

To call Kermode the finest English critic of his generation would be a __________ compliment, since not many of its population are professionally engaged in literary criticism.To call Kermode the finest English critic of his generation would be a __________ compliment, since not many of its population are professionally engaged in literary criticism.
答案：C, E　paltry; meager
backhanded
backhanded: standard unit headword
#71 SE
Between the late 1800s and early 1900s, various institutional structures emerged that set researchers in scientific fields apart as a professional class and moderated disputes by __________ some kinds of knowledge as real science.Between the late 1800s and early 1900s, various institutional structures emerged that set researchers in scientific fields apart as a professional class and moderated disputes by __________ some kinds of knowledge as real science.
答案：B, F　sanctioning; endorsing

#72 SE
Experiments show that it is shockingly easy to elicit a sense of __________ among a group of strangers: just tell them they’ll be working as a team, and they immediately start working as a team.Experiments show that it is shockingly easy to elicit a sense of __________ among a group of strangers: just tell them they’ll be working as a team, and they immediately start working as a team.
答案：D, E　solidarity; camaraderie
solidarity

Although we no longer have to rely on salt to keep food from spoiling, our appetite for it is __________: most people eat much more salt than they need.Although we no longer have to rely on salt to keep food from spoiling, our appetite for it is __________: most people eat much more salt than they need.
答案：C, E　unabated; undiminished
insatiable
insatiable: standard unit headword

Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can __________ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can __________ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.
答案：A, F　hamper; impede

答案：A, B　indifference; apathy

答案：A, C　sparse; paltry

Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)__________—he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)__________ force in the development and production of a movie.The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)__________ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)__________—he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)__________ force in the development and production of a movie.

A child who is going to lie must recognize the truth, intellectually conceive of an alternate reality, and be able to convincingly sell that new reality to someone else. Therefore, lying (iii)__________ both cognitive development and social skills that honesty simply doesn’t require.Given children’s active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as a young child’s (i)__________ virtue. It turns out that lying is the more (ii)__________ skill. A child who is going to lie must recognize the truth, intellectually conceive of an alternate reality, and be able to convincingly sell that new reality to someone else. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　an acquired; advanced; demands

#104 TC SINGLE
Instead of demonstrating the __________ of archaeological applications of electronic remote sensing, the pioneering study became, to some skeptics, an illustration of the imprudence of interpreting sites based on virtual archaeology.Instead of demonstrating the __________ of archaeological applications of electronic remote sensing, the pioneering study became, to some skeptics, an illustration of the imprudence of interpreting sites based on virtual archaeology.
答案：C　promise
archaeology
archaeology: standard unit headword
#105 TC TRIPLE
Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)__________ scientific model is (ii)__________ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)__________ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)__________ scientific model is (ii)__________ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)__________ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate
given
given: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　diffident; assertiveness
diffident

He defended the governments equivocations regarding the mysterious contrail as resulting not from __________ as critics say, but from a benightedness about its own doings that is almost inevitable, given the multitudinousness of its agencies.He defended the governments equivocations regarding the mysterious contrail as resulting not from __________ as critics say, but from a benightedness about its own doings that is almost inevitable, given the multitudinousness of its agencies.
答案：B　mendacity

#108 TC TRIPLE

They can, however, make it more (iii)__________ to communicate scientific developments, even to colleague.While some commentators suggest that abstraction and complexity in scientific research are signs that a given direction is misguided, Lisa Randall, a professor of physics at Harvard, counters that these qualities instead reflect the success of human ingenuity in (i)__________ the increasingly (ii)__________ challenges that nature presents. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　meeting; difficult; challenging

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　elitist; undesirable

答案：B　obscure

#112 TC DOUBLE
To the avid reader of E.O. Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i) __________, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii) __________ and ambition.To the avid reader of E.O. Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i) __________, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii) __________ and ambition.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　predictable; purview

答案：B　wherewithal

#114 TC TRIPLE
The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)__________ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Although viewed by many as (ii)__________ the healing process, these factors may actually provide an important clue as to why placebo often work: they are part of an experience that can itself be a (iii)__________ illness.The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)__________ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　get; incidental to; powerful antidote to

答案：C　surreptitious

答案：A　superficial

答案：C, F　decay; deterioration

The gain in body mass would make birds obese by human standards, and should render them unable to fly, but this gain is (i)__________ premigratory (ii)__________ of digestive organs, inactive during migration, and by the (iii)__________ of the muscles that power the flight.The longer the migratory route, the more fat migrating birds must accumulate as fuel for their flight. The gain in body mass would make birds obese by human standards, and should render them unable to fly, but this gain is (i)__________ premigratory (ii)__________ of digestive organs, inactive during migration, and by the (iii)__________ of the muscles that power the flight.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　partially offset by; shrinking; strengthening

Although this quality of Marson's poetry struck Sherlock as a sign of her emotional generosity (he remarked on her great "love of her homeland and its people"), Sherlock's (ii) __________ was not (iii) __________: he found some of Marson's poems excessive in their emotionality.Writing about Jamaican poet Una Marson in 1937, scholar Philip Sherlock (i) __________ the nationalistic feeling with which Marson's poems are imbued. 答案：A, E, I　noted; approbation; unqualified

#157 SE
All Shaker furniture implies __________ humanism in design: the Shakers made objects that look like objects, following a nonhuman law of design and rejecting the unconscious self-flattery inherent in making anthropomorphic objects.All Shaker furniture implies __________ humanism in design: the Shakers made objects that look like objects, following a nonhuman law of design and rejecting the unconscious self-flattery inherent in making anthropomorphic objects.
答案：A, B　a rejection of; a liberation from
self-flattery
self-flattery: standard unit headword

Nordhaus predicts that in the future we will increasingly be (i) __________ ecological problems like global warming rather than (ii) __________ them. We may, for example, make some headway in limiting emissions that contribute to warming, but much of our work will be in adapting to ecological problems and alleviating their effects.Nordhaus predicts that in the future we will increasingly be (i) __________ ecological problems like global warming rather than (ii) __________ them. We may, for example, make some headway in limiting emissions that contribute to warming, but much of our work will be in adapting to ecological problems and alleviating their effects.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　managing; solving
emission

Friedlander reminds us that everyday objects (ii) __________ or, more exactly, that the unimpeded visual scene of a building without cars or power lines or street signs is a highly (iii) __________ photographic artifact.Unlike those glossy Architectural Digest photographs that are deliberately (i) __________ lest they distract from a focus on the building itself, Friedlander's architectural photography is the product of his insistence that he is an individual looking at buildings and scenes as they actually exist. Friedlander reminds us that everyday objects (ii) __________ or, more exactly, that the unimpeded visual scene of a building without cars or power lines or street signs is a highly (iii) __________ photographic artifact.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　impersonal; normally interfere; contrived
contrive
contrive: standard unit headword

答案：E　germane to

Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)__________ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　get; incidental to; powerful antidote to

Many were uncomfortable at the way the corporation was forced to spend much of last year expanding, and this new venture is potentially even more risky.Even though the company's CEO professes to be (i) __________ and urges the deal forward, its investors are unlikely to be so (ii) __________ about its prospect. Many were uncomfortable at the way the corporation was forced to spend much of last year expanding, and this new venture is potentially even more risky.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　confident; sanguine
force
force: standard unit headword
#211 TC TRIPLE
Computers' triumph in chess had been engineered not by creating machines that (i)__________ human thought, as most experts in artificial intelligence had expected, but by perfecting machines that played like machines. The analogy with flight is (ii)__________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, attaching wings to their arms and flapping madly, they were (iii)__________ to fail. Once engineering escaped the paradigm of the familiar, however, people were soon flying much faster than birds.Computers' triumph in chess had been engineered not by creating machines that (i)__________ human thought, as most experts in artificial intelligence had expected, but by perfecting machines that played like machines. The analogy with flight is (ii)__________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, attaching wings to their arms and flapping madly, they were (iii)__________ to fail. Once engineering escaped the paradigm of the familiar, however, people were soon flying much faster than birds.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　aped; instructive; doomed
wing
wing: standard unit headword
#212 SE
Laboratory experiments testing whether memories can be purposely erased or implanted seem decidedly __________ compared to the sensational depictions of memory manipulation in movies, but such research has nevertheless yielded dramatic results.Laboratory experiments testing whether memories can be purposely erased or implanted seem decidedly __________ compared to the sensational depictions of memory manipulation in movies, but such research has nevertheless yielded dramatic results.
答案：D, F　prosaic; pedestrian
pedestrian
pedestrian: standard unit headword

答案：A　discursive

Frequently that time happened in a great classroom somewhere, in the hands of the one (ii) __________ teacher we can each remember.
Blank 1
Blank 2

The major (ii)__________ urbanization in the United States, the railway fell victim to it. Once the overwhelming majority of nonelective journeys were either very long or very short, it became (iii)__________ for people to undertake them in planes or cars.By sucking people into towns and draining the countryside of communities and workers, the passenger train (i)__________ its reason for being: moving people between country districts and urban centers. The major (ii)__________ urbanization in the United States, the railway fell victim to it. Once the overwhelming majority of nonelective journeys were either very long or very short, it became (iii)__________ for people to undertake them in planes or cars.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　destroyed; facilitator of; sensible

overwhelming: standard unit headword
#216 SE

答案：B, C　contrive; devise

答案：B　unambiguously

Closely spaced stop lights have been added on roads into town, causing delays. Pedestrian underpasses designed to allow traffic to flow freely across major intersections have been (ii) __________.

答案：A, C　hackneyed; pedestrian

#220 TC TRIPLE
The general statement that all objects are (i)__________ by gravity (ii)__________ is not (iii)__________. In reality, if a feather and a lump of iron were dropped at the same time from the same place, we should not expect them to hit the floor at the same time. In order to reconcile the general statement with real-world phenomena, we must take into consideration all of the forces that act upon objects.The general statement that all objects are (i)__________ by gravity (ii)__________ is not (iii)__________. In reality, if a feather and a lump of iron were dropped at the same time from the same place, we should not expect them to hit the floor at the same time. In order to reconcile the general statement with real-world phenomena, we must take into consideration all of the forces that act upon objects.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　accelerated; at the same rate; self-evident

force: standard unit headword

答案：A　misleading

答案：A, D　fade; wane

Yet the term "error" could be (i)__________ in that it suggests that there might be (ii)__________ way in which cognition should work. Such a binary opposition is (iii)__________.

Skeptics are (i) __________ deniers, who are ideologically committed to a position and (ii) __________ in the face of evidence.

#263 TC TRIPLE
Indira Gandhi is independent India's most puzzling political figure. She was enigmatic and often (i) __________ in person, and her political identity was no less (ii) __________. As Prime Minister, she seemed to be a woman of great (iii) __________, but privately she was diffident and self-doubting.Indira Gandhi is independent India's most puzzling political figure. She was enigmatic and often (i) __________ in person, and her political identity was no less (ii) __________. As Prime Minister, she seemed to be a woman of great (iii) __________, but privately she was diffident and self-doubting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　opaque; elusive; self-assurance
diffident
diffident: standard unit headword
#264 TC SINGLE
Barbara Johnson's 1982 essay on Mary Shelley's Frankenstein drew upon feminist texts written for a popular, rather than academic, audience without __________: Johnson's critique is dense and theoretically sophisticated.Barbara Johnson's 1982 essay on Mary Shelley's Frankenstein drew upon feminist texts written for a popular, rather than academic, audience without __________: Johnson's critique is dense and theoretically sophisticated.
答案：B　sacrificing rigor
opposition
opposition: standard unit headword
#265 SE
Because archaeology explores the most profound changes in human history by means of a grossly incomplete record, it has invited the sort of bold, imaginative interpretation in which speculation too easily becomes __________ evidence.Because archaeology explores the most profound changes in human history by means of a grossly incomplete record, it has invited the sort of bold, imaginative interpretation in which speculation too easily becomes __________ evidence.
答案：C, D　untethered from; divorced from
archaeology
archaeology: standard unit headword
#266 TC DOUBLE
At its best, the storytelling in this television series manages to accommodate a sense of historical (i) __________. The narrative shows that American independence was not (ii) __________. It was debated. It didn't happen overnight. No one was sure how it would turn out.At its best, the storytelling in this television series manages to accommodate a sense of historical (i) __________. The narrative shows that American independence was not (ii) __________. It was debated. It didn't happen overnight. No one was sure how it would turn out.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　contingency; inevitable
narrative
narrative: standard unit headword
#267 TC TRIPLE
History is about recognizing continuities and therefore the historian must (i) __________ stories that rope together the disparate worlds of past and present. But it is also true that honest history arises from a sense of (ii) __________. It is the experience of discontinuity that alerts us to the (iii) __________ of the past and sets us looking for narratives to capture that unfamiliar entity.History is about recognizing continuities and therefore the historian must (i) __________ stories that rope together the disparate worlds of past and present. But it is also true that honest history arises from a sense of (ii) __________. It is the experience of discontinuity that alerts us to the (iii) __________ of the past and sets us looking for narratives to capture that unfamiliar entity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　weave; interruptedness; strangeness
narrative
narrative: standard unit headword
#268 TC SINGLE
The detours taken in the course of the essay, which could easily have been __________, instead proved to be a virtue because they epitomized the instability of a period characterized by constant experimentation and change.The detours taken in the course of the essay, which could easily have been __________, instead proved to be a virtue because they epitomized the instability of a period characterized by constant experimentation and change.
答案：A　perplexing
edifying
edifying: standard unit headword
#269 SE
Despite the general __________ of Roman archaeological studies toward the major cities and their monuments, archaeology has contributed much to a better understanding of rural developments in Roman territory.Despite the general __________ of Roman archaeological studies toward the major cities and their monuments, archaeology has contributed much to a better understanding of rural developments in Roman territory.
答案：E, F　bias; orientation
archaeology
archaeology: standard unit headword
#270 TC DOUBLE
Although people sometimes conflate them, the Beat poetry scene and the folk-music revival were actually (i) __________ and in many ways (ii) __________, despite sharing certain ancestral connections in Depression-era left-wing politics.Although people sometimes conflate them, the Beat poetry scene and the folk-music revival were actually (i) __________ and in many ways (ii) __________, despite sharing certain ancestral connections in Depression-era left-wing politics.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　distinct; antagonistic
wing
wing: standard unit headword
#271 TC TRIPLE
The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity
force
force: standard unit headword
#272 TC SINGLE
While her coworkers used a different excuse every year to shirk the annual company picnic, Donna was a consistent __________, feigning illness every year.While her coworkers used a different excuse every year to shirk the annual company picnic, Donna was a consistent __________, feigning illness every year.
答案：B　malingerer
maverick
maverick: standard unit headword
#273 SE
During the eighteenth century, improvements in their material circumstances did not necessarily mean expanded independence for women of elite families and, arguably, the social conventions of gentility __________ more of their time and energy.During the eighteenth century, improvements in their material circumstances did not necessarily mean expanded independence for women of elite families and, arguably, the social conventions of gentility __________ more of their time and energy.
答案：C, E　demanded; exacted

elite: standard unit headword
#274 TC DOUBLE
The technology of integrated circuits inaugurated a new era of electronics, which can be characterized by saying that any electronic circuit, however (i) __________, that can be designed can also be made reliably and cheaply, provided it is manufactured and sold in sufficient numbers to (ii) __________ the cost of design and production.The technology of integrated circuits inaugurated a new era of electronics, which can be characterized by saying that any electronic circuit, however (i) __________, that can be designed can also be made reliably and cheaply, provided it is manufactured and sold in sufficient numbers to (ii) __________ the cost of design and production.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　complicated; recover
production
production: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　change in; defensible; reduce the tendency toward

given: standard unit headword

答案：E　cynicism

答案：A, B　presage; foretell

答案：B, F　recalcitrant; restive

Blank 2

Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)__________ that other uses of the act can (ii)__________ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)__________.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　fostered the misconception; duplicate; pertain

答案：D, E　mendacious; deceitful

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　the gold standard; expense

#285 TC TRIPLE
A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)__________. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)__________ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)__________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)__________. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)__________ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)__________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　understandable; arousal; breakthrough
torpor
torpor: standard unit headword
#286 SE
In contrast to relatively __________ lunar events, such as lunar eclipses, that are timed in minutes or hours, planets typically remain on display in the sky for weeks or months.In contrast to relatively __________ lunar events, such as lunar eclipses, that are timed in minutes or hours, planets typically remain on display in the sky for weeks or months.
答案：B, E　transitory; ephemeral

They are running animals and/or flying animals, but only a few use their legs and wings (ii) __________, as climbing mammals routinely do with legs and arms.Compared with mammals, birds could be described, in Tudge's words, as being more (i) __________ in their construction. Birds are like several animals in one. They are running animals and/or flying animals, but only a few use their legs and wings (ii) __________, as climbing mammals routinely do with legs and arms.

Blank 2
答案：A, D　modular; in concert

(ii)__________ by the (iii)__________ of the volunteer, the Galaxy Zoo team was inspired to pursue lines of research they had never even imagined.Galaxy Zoo set a standard for citizen-scientist participation project. Zealous volunteers (i)__________ the project’s organizers by classifying an entire catalog of galaxies years ahead of schedule. (ii)__________ by the (iii)__________ of the volunteer, the Galaxy Zoo team was inspired to pursue lines of research they had never even imagined.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　astonished; Buoyed; alacrity

答案：B, D　nuance; subtlety

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　tendentious; perverse

#291 TC TRIPLE
Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)__________ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)__________ individual factors and thus (iii)__________ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)__________ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)__________ individual factors and thus (iii)__________ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　trigger; focus on; overlook

答案：C, D　drive; suspense

After all, similar frenzies have been in other art forms in periods of their rapid development, for instance, the debates about painting that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii) __________ rarely precedes creative glory. On the contrary, it commonly indicates that an artistic (iii) __________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasing strained emulation and diminishing returns has set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzy about comics today is (i) __________. After all, similar frenzies have been in other art forms in periods of their rapid development, for instance, the debates about painting that roiled Renaissance Italy. On the contrary, it commonly indicates that an artistic (iii) __________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasing strained emulation and diminishing returns has set in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　understandable; arousal; breakthrough

Furthermore, even if new oil finds elsewhere have been (ii) __________ than in the past, substantial quantities of oil can be profitably stripped from tar and shale.

#308 TC TRIPLE
The order applies to all Federal agency whose actions may affect the status of invasive species and requires agencies to identify such actions and, to the extent practicable and permitted by law, since invasive species severely reduce the number of native species and even (i)__________ their existence, the agency has determined and made public its determination that the benefits of such actions clearly outweigh the potential harm caused by invasive species; and that all feasible and (ii)__________ measures to (iii)__________ risk of harm of the introduction of invasive species will be taken in conjunction with the actions.The order applies to all Federal agency whose actions may affect the status of invasive species and requires agencies to identify such actions and, to the extent practicable and permitted by law, since invasive species severely reduce the number of native species and even (i)__________ their existence, the agency has determined and made public its determination that the benefits of such actions clearly outweigh the potential harm caused by invasive species; and that all feasible and (ii)__________ measures to (iii)__________ risk of harm of the introduction of invasive species will be taken in conjunction with the actions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　preclude; prudent; minimize

#309 TC DOUBLE

MRI scans of an elephant's brain suggest that even relative to its overall size it has a large hippocampus, the component in the mammalian brain linked to memory and an important part of its limbic system, which is involved in processing emotions.There has been (i) __________ elephant's fabled mental capacities until recently, when these behavioral observations have begun to be (ii) __________ by brain science. MRI scans of an elephant's brain suggest that even relative to its overall size it has a large hippocampus, the component in the mammalian brain linked to memory and an important part of its limbic system, which is involved in processing emotions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　only anecdotal evidence for; buttressed

答案：C, D　sympathetic; well-disposed

Certainly a good science education can (ii) __________ habits of mind, but the (iii) __________ effect of education in nonscientific, humanistic subjects such as literature and history should not be underrated.Those who blame the inadequacies of science education for students' lack of reasoning skills perpetuate their unproductive notion of science as (i) __________, unique in its capacity to inoculate us against superstition and ignorance. Certainly a good science education can (ii) __________ habits of mind, but the (iii) __________ effect of education in nonscientific, humanistic subjects such as literature and history should not be underrated.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　salvational; counter uncritical; salutary

But thinking that everyone is, and should be, an entrepreneur can encourage a tendency to (ii) __________ the virtues of larger businesses and the income that a steady job can provide.
Blank 1

答案：C, F　cult; underrate

答案：C, D　assumed; counted on

#314 TC TRIPLE

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　encourage; behemoths; preeminence

maverick: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　descriptive; a normative

答案：B, F　correcting; redressing

By contrast, the same strains exhibit such ecological (ii)__________ that they would be considered (iii)__________ species by normal ecological criteria.Strains of Prochlorococcus plankton have such (i)__________ sequences at their small subunit RNA genes that they would be considered the same species by the normal criteria used by microbiologists. By contrast, the same strains exhibit such ecological (ii)__________ that they would be considered (iii)__________ species by normal ecological criteria.

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　austere; largesse

答案：B, D　abjure; repudiate

For whom is Shipton's A New History of Jazz intended? It is written in (i)__________ language and contains no extended musical analysis, suggesting that it is meant for amateurs. But as it is twice as long as the next-longest history of the subject, my guess is that general readers will be put off by its (ii)__________. On the other hand, its great length is precisely what enables Shipton to avoid (iii)__________ the story of jazz's development.For whom is Shipton's A New History of Jazz intended? It is written in (i)__________ language and contains no extended musical analysis, suggesting that it is meant for amateurs. But as it is twice as long as the next-longest history of the subject, my guess is that general readers will be put off by its (ii)__________. On the other hand, its great length is precisely what enables Shipton to avoid (iii)__________ the story of jazz's development.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　accessible; proliferation of detail; oversimplifying

Yet the true believers have (iii) __________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views and continuing to lean on supporting research that was retracted long ago.Belief in the harmful effects of the drug has all the hallmarks of a viewpoint that is not (i) __________ even though the notion that the drug has negative consequences has been (ii) __________ multiple epidemiological studies over the past decade. Blank 3

His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　restraining; plausible; blunder

It is worth bearing in mind, however, that infestations of insects and disease are typically (i) __________ events, whereas weeds are (ii) __________.

The same is probably true for Mars; nevertheless, as the martian landscape is not as (i) __________ as the terrain on Earth, with no plate tectonics, no vegetation, less water, and lower erosion rates, the landscape's (ii) __________ the climate on Mars could be (iii) __________.It has been demonstrated that on Earth, no single landscape feature can be used as a quantitative indicator of past climatic conditions. The same is probably true for Mars; nevertheless, as the martian landscape is not as (i) __________ as the terrain on Earth, with no plate tectonics, no vegetation, less water, and lower erosion rates, the landscape's (ii) __________ the climate on Mars could be (iii) __________.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　complex; connection to; more direct

When a critic peremptorily announces that Titian's canvases are the greatest ever painted or that Mondrian and Malevich are the only truly radical artists of the twentieth century, we enjoy seeing all the confusions of taste swept aside, even though we may (ii) __________ reservations about the (iii) __________ nature of these opinions.There is something seductive about (i) __________ judgments in the arts. Common millet is easy to cultivate and appears to have been better suited to primitive agricultural practices than were other crops. #361 TC TRIPLE
Preservation of historically significant architecture once (i) __________. But as preservation's scope has widened from individual buildings to entire urban districts, it has become a cultural (ii) __________. Much of the debate involves modernist architecture's role in areas preserved as historic districts. Although preservation professionals often (iii) __________ modernist additions to these settings, there is strong public support for keeping new buildings traditional.Preservation of historically significant architecture once (i) __________. But as preservation's scope has widened from individual buildings to entire urban districts, it has become a cultural (ii) __________. Much of the debate involves modernist architecture's role in areas preserved as historic districts. Although preservation professionals often (iii) __________ modernist additions to these settings, there is strong public support for keeping new buildings traditional.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　provoked little controversy; battleground; advocate
opposition
opposition: standard unit headword
#362 SE
Despite its best efforts to stimulate sales, the bookselling business remains far from __________, for it has high fixed costs in wages and rent, and falling prices make these ever harder to spur.Despite its best efforts to stimulate sales, the bookselling business remains far from __________, for it has high fixed costs in wages and rent, and falling prices make these ever harder to spur.
答案：B, D　healthy; robust

parsimonious: standard unit headword
#363 TC TRIPLE
Pioneering medical research scientists’ (i)__________ claims regarding the (ii)__________ new researches make the public wonder why their own doctors are not dispensing miracles. There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that most require them to (iii)__________. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.Pioneering medical research scientists’ (i)__________ claims regarding the (ii)__________ new researches make the public wonder why their own doctors are not dispensing miracles. There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that most require them to (iii)__________. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　inflated; potential benefits of; oversell

force: standard unit headword | improvise: standard unit headword

答案：C, F　primitive; rudimentary

#367 TC TRIPLE
Compared with, say, the precision of a skein of geese winging its way across the autumn sky in a V formation, the seasonal marches of grazers across the Serengeti seem (i)__________, and at times even (ii)__________. But, in fact, years of careful observation by scientists have shown that there is (iii)__________ those migrations of zebras, wildebeests, and Thomson’s gazelles.Compared with, say, the precision of a skein of geese winging its way across the autumn sky in a V formation, the seasonal marches of grazers across the Serengeti seem (i)__________, and at times even (ii)__________. But, in fact, years of careful observation by scientists have shown that there is (iii)__________ those migrations of zebras, wildebeests, and Thomson’s gazelles.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　disorganized; chaotic; a definite order to

答案：A, F　burdensome; onerous

#369 TC TRIPLE
Given our species' increasing numbers and appetites--which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change--even (i)__________ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)__________, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)__________.Given our species' increasing numbers and appetites--which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change--even (i)__________ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)__________, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　now common; obligatory; jettisoned

contrive: standard unit headword | given: standard unit headword
#370 SE
The concert hall’s suspended ceiling is two-inch-thick plaster that reflects low-frequency sound energy; similarly, all wall surfaces are angled and shaped to __________ sound from the stage throughout the audience area.The concert hall’s suspended ceiling is two-inch-thick plaster that reflects low-frequency sound energy; similarly, all wall surfaces are angled and shaped to __________ sound from the stage throughout the audience area.
答案：A, C　diffuse; spread

eclipse: standard unit headword
#371 TC TRIPLE
Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious

答案：E, F　essential for; indispensable for

Having displayed his art collection in a vast modernist white space in (i)__________ former warehouse, Mr. Saatchi has chosen for his new site its polar opposite, a riverside monument to civic pomposity that once housed the local government. There is nothing (ii)__________ about the new location: the building's design is bureaucratic baroque, (iii)__________ style that is as declamatory as a task-force report and as self-regarding as a campaign speech.Having displayed his art collection in a vast modernist white space in (i)__________ former warehouse, Mr. Saatchi has chosen for his new site its polar opposite, a riverside monument to civic pomposity that once housed the local government. There is nothing (ii)__________ about the new location: the building's design is bureaucratic baroque, (iii)__________ style that is as declamatory as a task-force report and as self-regarding as a campaign speech.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　an unprepossessing; spare; a grandiose

Although evolutionary psychologists do not seem quite as imperialist in their intellectual ambitions as their sociobiologist forebears of the 1970s, they tend, in some critics’ view, to be no less __________ in their claims.Although evolutionary psychologists do not seem quite as imperialist in their intellectual ambitions as their sociobiologist forebears of the 1970s, they tend, in some critics’ view, to be no less __________ in their claims.
答案：B, D　arrogant; hubristic

#379 TC TRIPLE
The recent finding is particularly (i) __________, given that climatologists' predictions about sea-level changes are based on the expectation that if the ice sheet lost mass at its edges, its overall mass would increase, since rising temperatures would lead to more snowfall over the continent's midsection. However, if overall mass is actually (ii) __________, it will mean that climatologists' predictions for the rise in the sea level for the coming century are (iii) __________.The recent finding is particularly (i) __________, given that climatologists' predictions about sea-level changes are based on the expectation that if the ice sheet lost mass at its edges, its overall mass would increase, since rising temperatures would lead to more snowfall over the continent's midsection. However, if overall mass is actually (ii) __________, it will mean that climatologists' predictions for the rise in the sea level for the coming century are (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　ominous; decreasing; seriously understated
convincinggiven
convincing: standard unit headword | given: standard unit headword

The research informing Gregory’s book on vegetarianism in Victorian England appears to be __________, with a great deal of revealing detail on display and more than a third of the text taken up with footnotes.The research informing Gregory’s book on vegetarianism in Victorian England appears to be __________, with a great deal of revealing detail on display and more than a third of the text taken up with footnotes.
答案：C, E　exhaustive; comprehensive

uneven: standard unit headword
#381 SE
Though the art of writing was well established in Egypt and Mesopotamia long before the Shang period, the Chinese writing that appeared then seems to have been an entirely __________ development.Though the art of writing was well established in Egypt and Mesopotamia long before the Shang period, the Chinese writing that appeared then seems to have been an entirely __________ development.
答案：C, F　homegrown; indigenous

答案：A, D　affecting; moving

答案：A, D　Dearth; paucity

答案：C, D　an unfolding; a living

答案：C, E　ungainly; awkward

答案：B, E　origin; provenance

答案：B, D　ubiquitous; pervasive

答案：D, E　mitigate; curtail
